special_mechanics.name: Vehicle Acceleration
special_mechanics.description: Tune-up items provide turbo acceleration boost for higher performance

physics_values.max_achievable_speed: 127 mph (documented for AI vehicle at track 32)

special_mechanics.name: Top Speed Enhancement
special_mechanics.description: Hotter engines collectible power-up increases maximum vehicle top speed

special_mechanics.name: Traction & Cornering
special_mechanics.description: Super sticky tires tune-up item improves vehicle handling and turning

special_mechanics.name: Speed Boost Zones
special_mechanics.description: Stationary zippers on track provide extra speed boost when driven over

special_mechanics.name: Hazard Physics - Oil Slicks
special_mechanics.description: Oil slicks on track cause vehicles to spin out of control

special_mechanics.name: Hazard Physics - Water/Rain
special_mechanics.description: Water puddles and rain squalls slow down vehicle movement

tier_notes: Tier 2 - Qualitative data only. Wikipedia source provides gameplay mechanics and overview but lacks specific physics parameters. Game is a racing game featuring radio-controlled vehicles with power-ups and hazards rather than character platforming, so traditional jump/gravity mechanics do not apply. No technical documentation of sub-pixel system, acceleration curves, or precise velocity values available.
sources_used: wikipedia - R.C. Pro-Am article (covers series including R.C. Pro-Am II)
